expert_comparison.best_method_vs_expert: Median beats expert in 0.0% of conditions

key_findings: Median is the most effective aggregation method overall across all conditions tested.
A minimum of 10 agents is required for crowd aggregation to consistently outperform individual predictions.
Diversity level of 0.2 provides optimal aggregation performance. Too low diversity (0.2) results in correlated errors.
High correlation between predictions significantly degrades aggregation effectiveness.
Expert predictions outperform crowd aggregation in most conditions, suggesting limited wisdom of crowds effect in this setup.
Extremized aggregation shows particular strength with high-diversity crowds, effectively counteracting regression to the mean.
